creator: pauls@utdallas.edu
text:
--On January 30, 2007 2:22:52 AM +0300 Boris Samorodov <bsam@ipt.ru> wrote:

> I don't understand what I've done incorrectly and what should be done:
>
> 1. You are speaking about
> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=107895 ?
>
> 2. The patch from that PR is applied:
> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/cvsweb.cgi/ports/security/sguil-server/files/
> sguild.sh.in.diff?r1=1.1&r2=1.2&f=h
>
> Please, provide a patch against current port. Thanks.

Well, now I'm confused.  When I checked the port the other day, the patch 
had been placed in the port rather than applied to the init script. 
Tonight, when I checked the port, the patch was properly applied.

I guess you can close both PRs.

Paul Schmehl (pauls@utdallas.edu)
Senior Information Security Analyst
The University of Texas at Dallas
http://www.utdallas.edu/ir/security/
